question_text: Being assigned as a project manager, you noticed during project execution that conflicts arise in the team on both technical and interpersonal levels. What is an appropriate way of handling conflicts?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Conflicts distract the team and disrupt the work rhythm. You should always smooth them when they surface.
option_2: A conflict should be handled in a meeting so that the entire team can participate in finding a solution.
option_3: Conflicts should be addressed early and usually in private, using a direct, collaborative approach.
option_4: You should use your coercive power to quickly resolve conflicts and then focus on goal achievement.

question_text: What is the purpose of a project charter?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: To formally authorize a project or a phase and document initial requirements which satisfy the stakeholder's needs and expectations.
option_2: To document how the project will be planned, executed, monitored/controlled, and closed.
option_3: To link the project, which is going to be planned, executed, and monitored/controlled to the ongoing work of the organization.
option_4: To describe the process of performing the work defined in the project management plan in order to achieve the project's objectives.

question_text: Which is a central objective of using a Kanban board?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Allowing for three-point estimation of work items
option_2: Limiting the number of items in the Work in Progress column
option_3: Allowing for Fibonacci numbers in estimation of epics
option_4: Giving the Scrum Master a tool to add stories to the backlog

question_text: What does the term continuous delivery often refer to?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: A standardized set of deliverables, like plans, reports, and checklists.
option_2: A set of tools and techniques that a project manager should master.
option_3: Increasing customer satisfaction by delivering valuable products and increments early.
option_4: A specific sequence of work, described in terms of soft logic.

question_text: The communications management plan is a document which includes descriptions of
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Project level performance reports
option_2: Activity level status reports
option_3: Stakeholder communication requirements
option_4: Project benefit analysis results

question_text: _________ are usually not a manifestation of unique organizational cultures and styles.
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Shared visions, values, norms, beliefs, and expectations
option_2: Individual traits and attitudes of co-workers
option_3: Views of authority relationships
option_4: Policies, methods, and procedures

question_text: Which of the following are generally regarded as elements of active listening?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Making eye contact
option_2: Pressurizing the speaker
option_3: Paraphrasing
option_4: Interpreting the information
reference: Project Management JumpStart by Kim Heldman, page 47.

question_text: As the project manager, you decided to arrange a team meeting to identify and analyze lessons learned from quality control with stakeholders. What should you do with them?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Document them and make them part of the historical database for the project and the performing organization.
option_2: Discuss them with management and make sure that they remain otherwise confidential
option_3: Publish them in the corporate newsletter.
option_4: Follow your strategic decisions, independent from lessons learned. These decisions have been made and should be implemented whatever the outcomes are.

question_text: Your Scrum Team is working with a Product Owner. During daily Scrum meetings, the person criticizes your team's perceived "dysfunctionality" and requires that one developer should be delegated to take the lead? What could be an appropriate response to that? (Choose 2)
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: The Product Owner's job is to guide the direction of the product.
option_2: The Product Owner's job is to validate the final deliverables of the project.
option_3: The Product Owner's job is to verify that the rules of Scrum are adhered to.
option_4: The Product Owner should not join the Daily Scrums as he/she is not a member of the Scrum Team.
option_5: The Product owner manages the product backlog for the Developer Team.

question_text: How should change management be planned for?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Changes are generally not predictable, therefore planning for change management cannot be reasonable.
option_2: Planning for change management should be done while the various change control processes are being applied.
option_3: Change management can be planned in a set of management plans or a specific change management plan.
option_4: Changes are a sign of bad planning. One should avoid changes during a project, thus eliminating the need to manage them.

question_text: According to Bruce Tuckman, what are the stages of team development?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Honeymoon, rejection, regression, acceptance, re-entry
option_2: Forming, storming, norming, performing (adjourning)
option_3: Tell, sell, consult, join
option_4: Direct, support, coach, delegate
reference: Project Management JumpStart by Kim Heldman, page 214.

question_text: What is not a reason for companies to organize lessons learned?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Lessons learned databases are an essential element of the organizational process assets.
option_2: Lessons learned should focus on identifying those accountable for errors and failures.
option_3: Lessons learned sessions should bring about recommendations to improve future performance on projects.
option_4: Phase-end lessons learned sessions provide a good team building exercise for project staff members.

question_text: How does a project management team stay in touch with the work and the attitudes of project team members?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: By observation and communication
option_2: Using closed questions during team meetings
option_3: Through third-party assessments
option_4: Through the team members' functional managers

question_text: Your project is running into a number of issues that could have been predicted using a formal risk management process with appropriate documentation Which document could have helped you and should have been developed along the risk management processes from identify risks through plan and implement risk responses to monitor risks?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: List of risk triggers
option_2: Risk register
option_3: Risk mitigation
option_4: Decision tree

question_text: A customer is requiring a minor scope change and expects you to do this without delays and additional costs. You believe that you have adequate authorization to make the decision by yourself, but you are not quite sure. What should be your next steps?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: A requested change is always an opportunity to get more money paid by the customer and to secretly solve schedule and quality problems. You should make some reasonable estimates on time, costs, risks etc. and then add a nice margin on top of that to calculate the new price.
option_2: Customer satisfaction is your top priority. The customer gives you an opportunity to increase their satisfaction, which you should use to the maximum benefit. Most project managers have contingencies to cover risks; these can be used to pay the additional costs.
option_3: Before making a decision you should have a look at the customer's parking lot. If you find there many expensive, new models, it is likely that you can use the requested change to increase the profit from the contract. Otherwise you should reject the request.
option_4: Handle the request according to the integrated change control processes described in your management plans. Then make a decision together with the appropriate change control body, whether the increased customer satisfaction will be worth the extra costs, work, risks etc.

question_text: A facilitator should…
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: …be in full control of the discussion and its outcomes.
option_2: …always take notes by herself.
option_3: …avoid a flip chart parking lot.
option_4: …give guidance as required without interfering.
reference: Project Management, A Systems Approach to Planning, Scheduling and Controlling by Harold Kerzner, 12th edition, page 226.

question_text: As the project manager in a software project which is currently initiated, you want to assess high- level risks. What should you do?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Develop the project charter and a risk management plan to start identifying risks based on those and other documents.
option_2: Identify and analyze risk events using qualitative and quantitative techniques.
option_3: Develop contingency plans and fallback plans in case the original plan proves wrong.
option_4: Discuss the risks documented in your Risk register with the project key stakeholders.

question_text: In most jurisdictions, a legally required order of formal statements establishing a contract is:
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Invitation to treat, offer, PO
option_2: Inquiry, bid, order
option_3: Offer, acceptance
option_4: MoU, LoI, final agreement
reference: Project Management, A Systems Approach to Planning, Scheduling and Controlling by Harold Kerzner, 12th edition, pages 661ff.

question_text: What should managers consider before conducting a performance evaluation interview with a project team member?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Which management fallacies can most easily be delegated to the worker?
option_2: How can discussion of the manager's leadership style be avoided?
option_3: Has the employee been provided with sufficient instructions and work tools?
option_4: How can dispute related to unsatisfactory performance be avoided?
reference: PMBOK® Guide 6th Edition, pages 142, 310; Successful Interviewing by Diane Arthur, page 154.

question_text: During team development, the handling of assumptions is causing problems. Some team members reject the notion that assumptions are an expression of uncertainty. Others recommended to make exploring assumptions an essential element of team building, following the Drexler/Sibbet Team Performance Model Which statement describes best the handling of assumptions in this model?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: It is the responsibility of the sales person in charge to identify all risks related to a customer project.
option_2: Managing and organizing assumptions means avoiding risks right from the start of the project.
option_3: Finding out more about assumptions should be an element of goal clarification by the team..
option_4: Risks are a sign of uncertainty. Avoiding all uncertainties means that a project should have no risks at all.

question_text: A WBS dictionary is a document which…
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: … describes technical terms used for scope management.
option_2: … describes the details for each component in the WBS.
option_3: … translates essential WBS terms for global project teams.
option_4: … helps translate functional into technical requirements.

question_text: You are in the process of contacting sellers to obtain bids and proposals. You found out that it might become a time-consuming procedure to ensure that the sellers get a clear and common understanding of the procurement process and of the needs of your project. Which technique may help you save time?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Bidder conferences
option_2: Advertising
option_3: Qualified sellers list
option_4: Four-eyes meetings

question_text: While you implement the plans of your project, you find that cultural differences and incompatibilities among your team members cause issues and resolving them leads to delays. As a project manager, when should you especially consider cultural differences? (Choose 2)
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: When you break down scope to create a work breakdown structure (WBS).
option_2: When you assign a human resources to do the work in a schedule activity.
option_3: When you develop acceptance criteria for work results to be achieved by the team members.
option_4: When you decide upon recognition and awards during team development.
option_5: When you have a globally distributed project team.

question_text: At the beginning of project execution, you notice different opinions between team members relating to project work and deliverables and to the level of overall complexity. What should you do right now?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Give your team members some time to develop a common understanding of the project scope and product scope. Upcoming interface problems may be resolved later.
option_2: Use the risk management processes to identify and assess risks caused by misunderstandings and develop a plan with measures in order to respond to them.
option_3: Organize meetings to identify and resolve misunderstandings between team members in order to avoid interface problems, disintegration and costly rework early in the project.
option_4: Use interviews in private with each individual team member to inform them of your expectations and your requirements in an atmosphere of confidence.

question_text: You have just taken over as manager of a project that will create many benefits for the performing organization, but you sense a high level of resistance in various stakeholders right from the start. What is the most appropriate action to resolve the problem?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Develop a responsibility assignment matrix (RAM) which clearly shows the responsibility of each stakeholder for the various project activities and who needs to be consulted and informed.
option_2: Develop an organization diagram, which places each of the stakeholders at an appropriate position inside the project and allows for certain lines of communication while disallowing others.
option_3: Schedule a meeting with these stakeholders to present the project, discuss and establish ground rules, ensure their involvement and identify initial personal and organizational issues.
option_4: Do not talk to these stakeholders too much at this time, instead create faits accomplis, which will later force the stakeholders to support the project due to a lack of alternatives for them.

question_text: You are in the process of planning a project and found that stakeholders often have varying objectives and requirements. This makes it hard to come up with a plan with which all objectives will be met. What is probably most helpful to ensure common understanding?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Ask the project stakeholders to build focus groups in order to discuss and remedy conflicting interests.
option_2: Create an exhaustive document describing your point of view and ask stakeholders to review it.
option_3: Let each stakeholder write a statement of work, merge the documents to a project scope statement.
option_4: Use your authorization as the project manager to clearly prioritize the different objectives.

question_text: Projects frequently do not meet customer expectations for which of the following reasons?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Poor risk management by the contractor running the project
option_2: Poor technical ability of the contractor running the project for the customer
option_3: Technical inability and poor risk management by the contractor
option_4: Unclear scope definition by the customer for whom the project is performed
reference: Project Management, A Systems Approach to Planning, Scheduling and Controlling by Harold Kerzner, 12th edition, page 593.

question_text: You are the project manager for the development of a new type of power plant. Your project is making fast progress, and it is getting nearer to the day of product acceptance. Which technique will be most important for product acceptance?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Inspection
option_2: Quality audit
option_3: Impact analysis
option_4: Team review

question_text: You have a discussion with management in organizations that the costs of quality are hard to assign and to balance against each What are the cost types in modern quality management?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Costs of good quality – costs of bad quality
option_2: Planning costs – assurance costs – control costs
option_3: Planning costs – doing costs – checking costs – acting costs
option_4: Prevention costs – appraisal costs – failure costs

question_text: During the execution of a project to build a complex defense system, your team has run into a deep crisis. The project's goal and objectives have been challenging right from the start, but now you have discovered that your team members have become increasingly unaware of them. Being busy solving detail problems, they often fail to understand the overall requirements. Then they develop solutions which resolve issues in their area of limited responsibility, while causing new problems at the interfaces with other system components. Meanwhile, the team members show signs of growing frustration, and time is running away. How can you help your team in this situation?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Accompany your team members along a sequence of maturity levels from dependence through independence to interdependence.
option_2: Ignore the low-level issues and focus on achieving the overall requirements, details can be sorted out during test and handover.
option_3: If everybody simply does their jobs, then there should be no problem. Make sure that team members are fully aware of their accountability.
option_4: Problems of this dimension have their origin in fundamental personal incompatibilities between team members. You should replace them all.
reference: The Human Aspects of Project Management: Managing the Project Team by Vijay K. Verma, Volume Three, page 70.

question_text: During execution in a project to build a major road bridge, your team found a major flaw in the technical drawings. On an ad-hoc base, they had to find and implement a workaround to avoid delays and mitigate technical problems. What should you do next?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Meticulously document the problem and the workaround to create a requested change to the project management plan, which will then be passed to the body responsible for the change control decision.
option_2: The workaround was performed on a technical level only. As long as it does not influence the function of the bridge or the organizational configuration of the project, a change request will not be necessary.
option_3: A retroactive formal change request side is not reasonable. Create some additional documents describing the workaround and sign them by yourself, you can present them during the next regular CCB meeting.
option_4: It is normal in projects that during project execution inconsistencies arise between planning documents and actual implementation. This is no problem as long as the functional status of the product is maintained.

question_text: You recently took over the assignment for a project. The project charter has been developed. What is an appropriate next step for you?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Develop the risk register.
option_2: Develop the project milestone schedule.
option_3: Develop management plans.
option_4: Get approval for the project management plan.

question_text: You scheduled a kick-off meeting in order to announce the start of your project, present the plan and develop consensus on it. Another topic will be explanation of each team member's role. In addition, you planned some time for discussions. You published the agenda of the kick-off meeting some weeks ago to all invited attendees. Now, short term before the event, you receive various cancellations by team members, who say that they cannot join the meeting. But they will be with you when project work will start.
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: The kick-off meeting is secondary, you are happy if the team members will do their jobs.
option_2: You should insist on the presence of the team members to the appointed date.
option_3: If you force team members' presence, they would anyway be distracted by their current problems.
option_4: It is probably a good idea to reschedule the meeting to a day, when all team members can attend.
reference: Management JumpStart by Kim Heldman, page 78.

question_text: During a meeting, a discussion came up: To which degree does your project have to be compliant to the processes enumerated in the PMBOK® Guide? What should be the guiding principle to answer this question?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: The processes are meant as rough guidelines to which a team should only adhere if there is enough consensus.
option_2: The more processes the project team follows, the better the project's performance will be.
option_3: The project manager must follow all 49 processes for project management without alterations.
option_4: The project team must select appropriate processes required to meet the project requirements.

question_text: Your project team is complaining that the acceptance criteria of their work result are unclear and ask you to provide clarification with the customer. You arrange a meeting, and several criteria are identified. The product acceptance criteria should now be laid down in which documents? (Choose 2)
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Project scope statement
option_2: Work breakdown structure
option_3: Resource assignments
option_4: Project scope management plan
option_5: An addendum to the project contract

question_text: During project execution you found out that work is often not performed at the right time and in the right order. Deliverables are made by one team member that cannot be processed by another one. This already leads to timely rework and bad morale among team members. Which project management tool is most appropriate to deal with problems like this?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Organization chart
option_2: RACI matrix
option_3: Communications management plan
option_4: Work authorization system
reference: PMBOK® Guide 6th Edition, page 59 Project Management, Sixth Edition by G. Michael Campbell, page 235.

question_text: Your management is applying the 10 percent solution: It has requested that you reduce the budget for the project which you are currently initiating by 10%. What will this probably mean for your project?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: If the budget is cut by 10%, then the project will have an accompanying degradation of quality by 10%.
option_2: If the project budget is an honest one, then a reduction in budget must be accompanied by a trade-off in either time or performance.
option_3: A 10% reduction in budget will be accompanied by a loss of performance much less than the target reduction in cost.
option_4: Most projects have "padded" estimates, and the budgetary reduction will force out the padding from the project.
reference: Project Management, A Systems Approach to Planning, Scheduling and Controlling by Harold Kerzner, 12th edition, page 575.

question_text: While applying procurement management processes in your project, you identified the need to gain a more objective understanding of sellers' capabilities in order to rank proposals and develop a negotiating sequence. Which technique may help avoid bias and subjective decisions best?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Bidder conference
option_2: Weighting system
option_3: Oral contract
option_4: Letter of intent
reference: Project Management, A Systems Approach to Planning, Scheduling and Controlling by Harold Kerzner, 12th edition, page 669.

question_text: During code inspection in a major software development project, a project management team identified frequent occurrences of critical programming errors. These errors are scattered across the code and occur without a discernible pattern. Which tool is most likely to help the team identify areas of error concentration in order to develop a prioritized response strategy?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Check sheet
option_2: Influence diagram
option_3: Decision tree
option_4: Process decision program chart

question_text: The term baseline is used in both disciplines project management and configuration management. Which statement is not true?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Baselines in system configurations are often also referred to as deltas.
option_2: In configuration management, baselines are used to control change.
option_3: Project management baselines are used for performance measurement.
option_4: In project management, baselines help assess variance and trends.

question_text: The scope baseline includes____.
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: The various management plans of the project
option_2: Schedule baseline and cost baseline
option_3: Product configuration ID and statement of work
option_4: WBS dictionary, WBS, scope statement

question_text: During human resource planning you identified that your team members are not sufficiently qualified for their tasks. Which may be an appropriate solution to this problem?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Reduce level of effort
option_2: Develop a training plan
option_3: Plan quality audits
option_4: Plan quality inspections

question_text: You are performing a customer project for a customer in a high-context cultural environment, while you and your team are in a low context country. Different communication styles and contents repeatedly led to conflicts between the organizations. What is typical for high-context cultures?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Technical means for communications can focus on transfer of spoken and written language.
option_2: Communication is preferred which avoids passing over a great deal of additional information.
option_3: A message has only limited meaning without an understanding of the surrounding context.
option_4: To understand a message, no information on history or personal opinions is needed.
reference: Mastering Virtual Teams: Strategies, Tools, and Techniques That Succeed by Deborah L. Duarte and Nancy Tennant Snyder, Second Edition, page 56.

question_text: In general, processes of configuration management do not include…
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: …identification and documentation of system or item characteristics.
option_2: …controlling changes to these characteristics in an integrative fashion.
option_3: …documenting and auditing changes and the implementation status.
option_4: …assigning responsibilities for change control and documentation.

question_text: The ________ is a document which describes how the project management team will implement the performing organization's quality policy.
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Quality assurance plan
option_2: Quality management plan
option_3: Quality commitment
option_4: Quality audit

question_text: What do you expect when you are submitting an invitation for bid to sellers?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Offers which will differ significantly in solution approach and price.
option_2: Offers which are similar enough to allow a selection based on price.
option_3: Offers which are not primarily written to win against competition.
option_4: General information on a seller's ability to provide the goods or services.
reference: Project Business Management by Oliver F. Lehmann, pages 91-92.

question_text: What is important for quality auditors?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: They must be in-house personnel.
option_2: They must be external-party staff.
option_3: They must be mandated by the auditee.
option_4: They must be properly trained.

question_text: Your agile team is uncertain for which elements of the project what methods should be applied. A recommendation was to follow the stages of uncertainty. Which are these stages?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Scope change and scope creep
option_2: Internal - political – economical – external – environmental
option_3: Simple – complicated – complex - chaos
option_4: Project complexity – product complexity

question_text: You are running a major project with four sub-projects. Each of the sub-project managers has developed a risk management plan, identified many risks, analyzed them and planned for risk response. It is now two months later. What should you not do?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Ensure that they handle all risks to save you from project level risk control.
option_2: Make sure that the sub project managers monitor their project work for new and changing risks.
option_3: Make sure that the sub project managers keep track of the identified risks and those on the watch list.
option_4: Make sure that the sub-project managers monitor trigger conditions for contingency plans.

question_text: What is true for statistical sampling?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Statistical sampling can be used to verify for each individual item in a lot, whether it can be accepted or must be rejected.
option_2: Attribute sampling means collection of quantitative data showing the level of conformity for each item of a sample.
option_3: Variables sampling includes collection of quantitative data on the degree of conformity for each item of a sample.
option_4: Statistical sampling is commonly used in quality assurance to examine if items of a lot conform to quality standards.

question_text: A project team was asked by a customer to shorten the cycle time of an iterative-incremental approach. What benefits may the customer hope to achieve?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Increased predictivity of the project
option_2: Shorter feedback loops
option_3: More frequent delivery
option_4: Easy reprioritization
option_5: Longer lead times for resource booking

question_text: What is not true for change requests?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Change requests should always be handled in a controlled and integrative fashion.
option_2: Change requests surpassing the formal change control processes can lead to scope creep.
option_3: Professionally managed, change requests can help improving a project and resolving emerging problems.
option_4: Change requests are always a sign of bad planning and should therefore be avoided.

question_text: You recently assigned staff members to activities, estimated work and scheduled your project. You then created the following resource histogram for your staff: What does this diagram tell you?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: The project can probably not be finished as scheduled.
option_2: You may be able to finish the project early by reassigning work.
option_3: You should avoid network logic diagramming for scheduling.
option_4: You may run into problems with dual reporting relationships. Project weeks 100% 0%

question_text: A project manager's PDM network schedule gets audited by a scheduling expert. The expert tells the project manager to focus more on nodes with "path convergence", also known as "sinks". What is the person referring to?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Nodes in a WBS, where several branches are brought back together.
option_2: Nodes in a conditional network diagram, at which it becomes deterministic.
option_3: Nodes in a decision tree, showing various branches with the same chance.
option_4: Nodes in a network logic diagram which have multiple predecessors.

question_text: The members of your project team have been assigned to your project with general availability levels of 50%. Yesterday, they reported to you that significant variances occurred during project execution. You have noted that the team members have been spending less than 50% of their time working on your project. Your project schedule is about to become heavily delayed, and deadlines are in jeopardy. What should you do?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Talk to your sponsor and try to get more resources assigned on similar conditions as the existing ones.
option_2: Talk with functional managers. Negotiate clear and written assignments with reliable scheduling priorities.
option_3: Ignore the difficulties, adjust your schedule and negotiate new deadlines according to the slower progress.
option_4: Focus on internal charges. Make sure that your project is not getting charged for more than the actual work.

question_text: What is the meaning of the acronym CSOW?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: A statement of work that is part of a contingency plan; it is commonly linked with Contingency reserves.
option_2: A statement of work that has been formally agreed upon by both parties and is therefore part of a contract.
option_3: A statement of work that is currently valid; this contrasts with the scope baseline, a formerly valid SOW.
option_4: A statement of work on project level that cumulates statements of work on cost account level
reference: Delivering Project Excellence with the Statement of Work by Michael G. Martin, page 37.

question_text: Crashing describes a technique to speed up a project by…

option_1: …reallocating existing resources or assigning additional resources to the project.
option_2: …overlapping activities which were originally planned to be done in sequence.

option_4: …reducing the duration estimates for activities to increase the pressure applied to the staff.

question_text: During risk identification sessions, you and your team identified more than 150 risks for your project. You are afraid that evaluating each of these risks quantitatively will cost you an enormous amount of time, while not all of them are really relevant and important enough to justify such a measure. What should you do next?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Identify risk triggers where possible. Analyze only those risks quantitatively for which no trigger could be found.
option_2: Use qualitative risk analysis in order to prioritize risks for further action, such as quantitative risk analysis.
option_3: Assess the probability of each risk qualitatively and further analyze only those risks with high probability.
option_4: Assess the potential impact of each risk qualitatively and further analyze only those risks with high impact.

question_text: During execution of a project, you observe that the performance of some of your team members is dropping, while others are doing a consistently good job. This is endangering overall project progress. What should you try first to bring the team as a whole back to performance?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Organize a team meeting and discuss openly the bad performance of the weak team members. Try to find a joint solution during the meeting.
option_2: Introduce a competitive incentive system with a bonus for the 20% of your team which is performing better than the other 80%.
option_3: Do not interfere, but give the team some time to organize and to sort the problem out themselves.
option_4: Research causes for bad performance, solicit systematic feedback and search for solutions based on that.

question_text: Your management announced that they want to put you on a stretch assignment. In this situation, you should consider that…
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: …a stretch assignment is going beyond your qualifications right from the start. Whatever your education, knowledge and skills are, you cannot fail personally. Congratulations.
option_2: …key stakeholders must have timely and complete information regarding your qualifications to make decisions regarding your suitability for the assignment.
option_3: …stretch assignments bear an increased risk of failure, and this is rarely outweighed by the learning and development experience expected to come with the assignment.
option_4: …you should avoid the increased hassle coming with the assignment. It will not be enough for you to simply satisfy requirements and achieve objectives. You will have to learn a lot.
reference: PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct, page 2 ( http://www.https://www.pmi.org/about/ethics/code).

question_text: Following the cultural approach of the corporation for which you are starting to manage a project, you are guiding your project team through the first life-cycle phase of conceptualization. Your next step is to call for a team meeting to let ideas emerge in an unrestricted creative process. What exactly should that look like?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: You write down the ideas of team members and structure them into categories of strengths, weaknesses, opportunities and threats during the session.
option_2: You creatively gather thoughts and ideas without any discussion or frowning. These ideas are only filtered and structured after the end of the brainstorming session.
option_3: Your team members wear hats with different colors, each of which stands for a way of thinking. Each member discusses topics from the viewpoint of their thinking.
option_4: You try to eradicate deviating ideas and concepts of team members during the meeting, so that the entire project team follows your principles.
reference: Project Management, A Systems Approach to Planning, Scheduling and Controlling by Harold Kerzner, 12th edition, page 223.

question_text: In configuration management, which of the following is necessary to maintain the integrity of baselines?

option_1: Analyzing variances by comparing baseline information with actual project data.

option_3: Ensuring that team members adhere to a code of ethical conduct and behavior.
option_4: Ensuring transparency across all project management processes and activities.

question_text: The sales representative of your company recently contacted you. You made some estimates on direct costs for an offer on a firm fixed-price contract. According to company policies, indirect costs (overheads) are to be calculated as a percentage of the direct costs, the sum of both cost types caused him to calculate a far too high price. How should you not react?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: You reduce your direct cost estimates by reducing the amount of work and the rates allocated to them. This allows a reduction of the overhead costs and a lower price.
option_2: Calculating overheads as a percentage of direct costs is just a model to simplify calculations. A more specific analysis of these costs may lead to a more realistic project price.
option_3: Cost estimates are quantitative assessments of the probable costs required to complete project work. These are made by the project team. It is the sales person's job to make the price.
option_4: You make the sales person aware that a reduction of direct costs may reduce the numbers for overhead cost allocation, but will not influence the actual overheads.

option_2: Passive risk avoidance
option_3: Passive risk acceptance
option_4: Active risk acceptance

choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions

question_text: In a lawsuit between your organization and a project contractor, the term "Constructive change" is discussed. Which statements on constructive changes are true?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Constructive changes are contract changes which are construed from actions taken by either party, not from a change document.
option_2: Changes in projects under contract are called constructive changes if they yield benefits for both parties.
option_3: Constructive changes are a frequent cause of disputes and claims of one contract party against the other.
option_4: Field changes should be meticulously documented to avoid an interpretation as constructive change.
option_5: Constructive change is a term used civil engineers for claims based on billable changes in building projects.

question_text: You are assigned as a project manager for an internal project. During cost planning and budgeting you discovered that there are peak times of costs during the project and other times when costs are fairly low. What could this mean for the project?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: The fiscal budget mechanism which is used by the performing organization may not allow for cost peaks in your project, and you may have to level out expenditures over time.
option_2: You can silently create budget contingencies during low-cost periods and re-use them later during those periods when you have to cover peak levels in project costs.
option_3: You may find another project manager with a project that has a different rhythm of high and low costs. Then, you may be able to move budget during one project's low cost periods to the other project.
option_4: You are a project manager and work with a lifecycle budget for your project. Fiscal budgets are a matter of the functional organization, and you generally do not have to care about them.

question_text: You are going to take over a new project as the project manager in an enterprise unknown to you. What should you investigate during the chartering process?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions

option_4: Team performance reports

choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions

option_3: Payment of an award fee would be agreed upon by both the customer and the contractor.

question_text: You took over a customer project for your company. From the inputs available, including contract, statement of work and project charter, you have developed a project management plan. You already presented that plan in a meeting with key stakeholders including your project sponsor and some representatives from the customer organization. During the meeting you sensed a high level of dissatisfaction by the customer executives, who signaled that the project might not produce the results that their company had expected. In your understanding, all necessary actions have been planned to meet the customer's requirements. What should you do next?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Request a written statement from the customer detailing the requirements which they believe are not addressed by your plan. Use this statement to update the project plan.
option_2: Arrange meetings with the customer to identify their needs, wants and expectations for the project. Then create a narrative Scope statement from this information to document the agreed upon project scope.
option_3: Request a formal meeting on top executive level to get the misunderstandings sorted out, then arrange a change request, re-plan your project where necessary and go ahead with the project work.
option_4: Do not over-react. Performed according to your plan, the project will produce a convincing product for the customer. As soon as the executives will see it, they will probably change their opinion and accept it.

question_text: What is not true for project deliverables?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Project deliverables should be identified, described and agreed upon as early in the project as possible.

question_text: Your project, performed for a customer, is coming to an end. The customer has been contractually granted a three years warranty period for the product of the project. What should you do first?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Hand all relevant documentation over to the organizational unit responsible for handling the warranty.

option_3: Changes on the product can affect warranty clauses. Ensure that these clauses are aligned with the final specifications.
option_4: According to many legislations, you cannot formally close a project before the end of the warranty period.

question_text: Being the project manager in a high-risk electronics project with a lot of new technologies, you developed a risk management plan and identified risks which you documented in a risk register. Then, the risks were analyzed and response was planned. During risk control meetings, it gets obvious that the documents you created are not very helpful. What have you probably done wrong?

option_4: You did not calculate EMVs.

question_text: Which is generally not regarded as one of the three categories of culture that managers should master?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: National culture

option_4: Functional culture

question_text: Post-mortem analysis after scheduled finish date of a project shows a CPI of 0.8 and an SPI of 1.25. What is a plausible explanation for that?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: The project was terminated early. At that time, it was over budget and ahead of schedule.

question_text: Some colleagues told you that they are planning, executing, monitoring and controlling a project by use of milestones only with durations between 1 and 4 weeks. What do you think?

option_4: It is a good approach because it saves from progress measurement on activities and work packages.

question_text: Together with your team, you applied three-point estimation on a cost item using a Beta distribution. The estimates (in USD) were made as - Optimistic: 15,000 - Most likely: 30,000 - Pessimistic: 75.000 What is the PERT value you would use?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions

question_text: You are just leaving a meeting during which you have been assigned as the manager of a project to build a sub-station that is part of a major electric power distribution system. choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Obtain a project charter which links the project to the strategy and ongoing work of the organization and documents the initial decisions.

option_3: Start developing a detailed risk register which includes identified risks with their qualitative and quantitative assessments and a response plan.
option_4: Start the quality assurance process by developing test procedures for the final deliverable and defining metrics against which the tests will be performed.

question_text: What is the purpose of the following diagram?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Describing the influence of extreme weather conditions on progress and on the achievement of project objectives.

question_text: During planning processes, you used Monte Carlo simulation to quantitatively assess cost and schedule risks of your project. During risk control, you repeat the technique, and it leads to different results. What should not be the reason for that?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: Some assumptions during planning have meanwhile become fact-based knowledge, so that the risks related to them have vanished or have become certain problems.

option_3: Some constraints have been identified originally, but their influence on the project was unclear when the simulation was run for the first time. By now, the team understands these constraints much better and has been able to adjust the simulation.
option_4: Some dummy activities in the network logic have an element of uncertainty, which gets bigger over time. While the project proceeds, it gets even harder to predict how the team members assigned to them will perform.

question_text: As a project manager you can assign any one of two team members to a highly coveted task. Both are equally capable, but one of them is a member of your in-group. To the other one, you have far more distance. How should you behave?
choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions
option_1: You disclose the situation to stakeholders and solicit a joint decision.
option_2: You take the person not in your in-group to avoid misunderstandings.
option_3: Chose the in-group person. The trustful relation will benefit the project.
option_4: You delegate the decision to a third team member to avoid the conflict.
reference: PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct, page 4 ( www.https://www.pmi.org/about/ethics/code).

question_text: For your agile product development to build a food processing plant, you need a batch of 100 identical valves which will be custom made for your project. question_text: You finished your project for a customer to implement a business software solution. All deliverables have been handed over and are already in use by the company. choice_label_scheme: 1..n in source answer key; options stored as ordered positions

question_text: At the end of an internal project, you find several key stakeholders including the sponsor reluctant to finally accept all deliverables and then close the project. What should not be your next step in this situation?
